mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets price in earnings improvements.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly changes with 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 1-year rolling window, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 6.0%, it marks a tightening labor market where rising incomes support consumption and confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In the 50 instances following UNRATE dropping below 6.0%, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is less than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of extremely low short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS near zero) and rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates negative real interest rates, which encourages equity investment as a hedge against cash erosion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase annualizes to above 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL change * inverse of FEDFUNDS) has a negative coefficient in predicting 60-day SPX returns (t-stat > -1.96) or the signal produces negative returns in more than 40% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals a strengthening manufacturing sector, which is especially bullish for equities during a post-shock recovery regime as capacity utilization improves and supply chains normalize, driving S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO increases >0.5% in a regime other than recovery, forward 60-day SPX returns are negative more often than positive; or the signal shows no significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.5 million units (annualized) indicate robust construction activity and housing demand, a leading indicator of economic strength that supports near-term equity gains as durable spending expectations rise, captured over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: More than 60% of 5-day windows following a HOUST >1500 print produce negative SPX returns, or the signal's hit rate is below 50% over a 2-year test.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are above 140 million and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains low (year-over-year below 2%), a strong labor market with stable prices provides an ideal backdrop for equity gains through rising disposable income and low discounting risk, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 140000 (thousands of persons) and CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2%
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce a positive average 20-day SPX return over 10 instances, or if the signal has a negative classification accuracy (F1 < 0.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remaining near zero (0.09%) signals an ultra-accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates, elevates risk appetite, and directly supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon as liquidity remains abundant.
failure_condition: On 5-day windows when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the S&P 500 return is negative more than 55% of the time, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent returns is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates at the same time as housing starts (HOUST) rise, it signals broad-based supply and demand alignment in the economy, reinforcing a virtuous cycle that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 gains over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal (INDPRO and HOUST both rising) does not predict positive SPX returns with a hit rate above 60% over a 2-year walk-forward period, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.10) in a regression onto forward returns.
